commands.review_summary:
The branch rewrites managed xAI/Grok `web_search` function tools and matching `tool_choice` entries to native `{ type: "web_search" }` for eligible Responses payloads while preserving configured non-xAI providers and policy-denied cases.

PR surface: Source +118, Tests +183. Total +301 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main keeps managed `web_search` for direct xAI-capable models and the xAI wrapper does not convert it before dispatch. I did not run a failing live xAI request in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No automated code repair is indicated; the remaining action is maintainer/live-provider validation for a compatibility-sensitive provider-routing change.

target.title: fix(xai): normalize grok web search tools
target.body:
## Summary

- Normalize xAI/Grok Responses payloads so a managed function tool named `web_search` is rewritten to xAI's native `{ type: "web_search" }` tool only when that managed tool is actually present.
- Preserve managed `web_search` when a different web-search provider is explicitly configured, when native web search is policy-denied, or when the request never included `web_search`.
- Keep forced and `allowed_tools` `tool_choice` constraints aligned by rewriting managed `web_search` choices to the native xAI web-search tool.

## External contract

- xAI's current Web Search docs show the Responses API native tool shape as `tools: [{ type: "web_search" }]`: https://docs.x.ai/developers/tools/web-search
- xAI's tools overview and advanced usage distinguish server-side tools from client-side function tools and describe server-side execution by xAI: https://docs.x.ai/developers/tools/overview and https://docs.x.ai/developers/tools/advanced-usage

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Grok/xAI Responses requests no longer carry duplicate or invalid managed `web_search` function definitions when xAI native search is the eligible final payload shape.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw worktree on Node 26 with repo wrappers after dependency install.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/xai/stream.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose -t "applies xai model compat"`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p extensions/xai/tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/xai/stream.ts extensions/xai/stream.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/xai/stream.ts extensions/xai/stream.test.ts`
- `node scripts/check-extension-plugin-sdk-boundary.mjs`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Evidence after fix: xAI stream wrapper tests pass with new cases for native conversion, configured non-xAI search provider preservation, absent-tool non-injection, policy-denied preservation, and `tool_choice` rewrite.

Observed result after fix: all local focused tests, lint, typecheck, format, boundary, diff, and autoreview checks listed above pass.

What was not tested: no live xAI API request was run; remote Crabbox/Testbox broad proof was unavailable because `crabbox`, `blacksmith`, and `../crabbox/bin` are absent in this environment.


---

## Senior review pass

Reviewed against current `main`, the live xAI Responses web-search contract, and the sibling `extensions/openai/native-web-search.ts`:

- **Contract is correct.** `{ type: "web_search" }` is the xAI Responses-API native web-search tool ([docs.x.ai](https://docs.x.ai/developers/tools/web-search)). `web_search_preview` and `search_parameters` are *not* xAI shapes, so rewriting the managed `web_search` function tool to the native tool (and forcing it via `tool_choice: { type: "web_search" }`, the OpenAI hosted-tool convention xAI inherits) is the right behavior. Cross-checked against grok web-search failure reports (#13494, #13010).
- **Cleanup applied** (`refactor(xai): type web search normalization off provider context`): read the native-search policy flag and `tools.web.search` config from the typed `ProviderWrapStreamFnContext` / `OpenClawConfig` instead of an `unknown` cast plus manual object navigation, matching the `openai` plugin. −20 LOC, no behavior change.

### Recommended follow-up (separate change)

This normalizer now duplicates the managed→native `web_search` predicates and provider-selection logic already in `extensions/openai/native-web-search.ts`. Per `extensions/CLAUDE.md` / `plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md` (a second consumer now exists), these belong in a shared `provider-stream-shared` family helper with both call sites migrated. Deferred from this focused fix because it touches the public Plugin SDK surface (`api-baseline`) and the OpenAI provider, warranting owner review rather than a drive-by.


---

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: xAI/Grok Responses payload normalization removes the managed function tool named `web_search` and leaves a single native server-side `{ "type": "web_search" }` tool, including forced `tool_choice` normalization.
- **Real environment tested**: local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `09d3f71d9b` in `/tmp/openclaw-grok-proof-94520`, Node.js v26.0.0, dependencies installed from the repo lockfile with `pnpm install --offline --ignore-scripts`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node --import tsx` executed the real `extensions/xai/stream.ts` `createXaiToolPayloadCompatibilityWrapper` against a Grok/openai-responses payload containing managed `web_search` plus `read`.
- **Evidence after fix**: terminal output from the live command:

```json
{
  "command": "node --import tsx live xai payload wrapper probe",
  "resultTools": [
    {
      "type": "function",
      "name": "read",
      "parameters": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {}
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "web_search"
    }
  ],
  "resultToolChoice": {
    "type": "web_search"
  },
  "managedWebSearchFunctionStillPresent": false
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: the final outbound tool list contains the normal `read` function tool and exactly one native xAI `web_search` server-side tool; the custom managed function named `web_search` is absent, so the Grok request no longer carries the invalid duplicate function definition.
- **What was not tested**: a live paid xAI API request was not sent from CI; this proof exercises the real OpenClaw xAI stream wrapper locally at the PR head.
